number): { atPos: number; query: string } | null {\n    // Find latest @ before cursor\n    let atPos = -1\n    for (let i = cursorPos - 1; i >= 0; i--) {\n        if (text[i] === '@') {\n      const afterAt = text.slice(atPos + 1, cursorPos)\n        // Allow spaces in mention queries, only reject newlines\n        if (!afterAt.includes('\\n')) {\n            return { atPos, query: afterAt }\n        }\n    }\n\n    return null\n}\n\n// Coords type for ProseMirror coordsAtPos\ntype Coords = { left: number; right: number; top: number; bottom: number }\n\n// Use basic ProseMirror schema directly\nexport const editorSchema = basicSchema\n\n// Mention state interface\nexport type MentionState = {\n    active: boolean\n    from: number // pos of \"@\"\n    to: number // current cursor\n    query: string\n} | null\n\nexport const mentionKey = new PluginKey<MentionState>('mention')\n\n// Mention detection plugin\nexport function mentionPlugin(opts: { onChange: (state: MentionState, coords?: Coords) => void }): Plugin {\n    return new Plugin<MentionState>({\n        key: mentionKey,\n        state: {\n            init: (): MentionState => null,\n            apply(tr, prev, _oldState, newState): MentionState {\n                const sel = newState.selection as TextSelection\n                if (!sel.empty) return null // Only handle cursor selection\n\n                const text = newState.doc.textBetween(0, newState.doc.content.size, '\\n')\n                const result = detectMentionAtCursor(text, sel.from - 1) // Adjust for paragraph node\n\n                if (!result) return null\n\n                return {\n                    active: true,\n                    from: result.atPos + 1, // Adjust for paragraph node\n                    to: sel.from,\n                    query: result.query,\n                }\n            },\n        },\n\n        props: {\n            decorations(state) {\n                const mention = mentionKey.getState(state)\n                if (!mention || !mention.active) return null\n\n                return DecorationSet.create(state.doc, [\n                    Decoration.inline(mention.from, mention.to, {\n                        class: 'pm-mention-active',\n                    }),\n                ])\n            },\n        },\n\n        view(editorView) {\n            // Check initial state immediately when view is created\n            const initialState = mentionKey.getState(editorView.state)\n\n            if (initialState && initialState.active) {\n                // Get coordinates for the \"@\" character position (from), not cursor (to)\n                try {\n                    const domCoords = editorView.coordsAtPos(initialState.from) as Coords\n                    opts.onChange(initialState, domCoords)\n                } catch (e) {\n                    // In test environment, coordsAtPos might fail - call onChange without coordinates\n                    opts.onChange(initialState, undefined)\n                }\n            }\n\n            return {\n                update(view, prevState) {\n                    const prev = mentionKey.getState(prevState)\n                    const curr = mentionKey.getState(view.state)\n\n                    // Only trigger onChange if state actually changed\n                    if (prev === curr) {\n                        return\n                    }\n\n                    if (curr && curr.active) {\n                        // Get coordinates for the \"@\" character },\n    })\n}\n\n// Helper function to apply suggestion selection\nexport function applySuggestion(view: any, suggestion: string) {\n    const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(view.state)\n    if (!mentionState) return\n\n    const { from, to } = mentionState\n    const newText = suggestion + ' '\n\n    // Safety check: ensure positions are within document bounds\n    if (from < 0 || to > The existing smoke\n * tests don't catch keyboard shortcut regressions.\n */\n\n// @vitest-environment jsdom\nimport { fireEvent, render } from '@testing-library/svelte'\nimport { describe, expect, test, vi } from 'vitest'\n\nimport PromptEditor from './PromptEditor.svelte'\n\n// Mock dependencies to focus on keyboard behavior\nvi.mock('$lib/telemetry', () => ({\n    TELEMETRY_RECORDER: {\n        recordEvent: vi.fn(),\n    },\n}))\n\nvi.mock('$lib/graphql', () => ({\n    getGraphQLClient: vi.fn(() => ({ query: vi.fn(() => Promise.resolve({ data: {} })) })),\n    getHeaders: vi.fn(() => ({})),\n}))\n\nvi.mock('$lib/wildcard', () => ({\n    getFileIconInfo: vi.fn(() => ({ icon: 'FileIcon', color: '#000' })),\n}))\n\nvi.mock('$lib/repo/shared/codehost', () => ({\n    getIconForCodeHost: vi.fn(() => 'RepoIcon'),\n}))\n\ndescribe('PromptEditor keyboard shortcuts', () => {\n    test('Enter key submits when content exists', async () => {\n        const onSubmit = vi.fn()\n        const { container } = render(PromptEditor, {\n            value: 'test query',\n            onSubmit,\n        })\n\n        const editor = container.querySelector('[data-testid=\"prompt-editor-textarea\"]') as HTMLElement\n        expect(editor).toBeTruthy()\n\n        if (editor) {\n            editor.focus()\n            await fireEvent.keyDown(editor, { key: 'Enter', code: 'Enter' })\n        }\n\n        expect(onSubmit).toHaveBeenCalledOnce()\n    })\n\n    test('Ctrl+Enter submits when content exists', async () => {\n        const onSubmit = vi.fn()\n        const { container } = render(PromptEditor, {\n            value: 'test query',\n            onSubmit,\n        })\n\n        const editor = container.querySelector('[data-testid=\"prompt-editor-textarea\"]') as HTMLElement\n        expect(editor).toBeTruthy()\n\n        if (editor) {\n            editor.focus()\n            await fireEvent.keyDown(editor, { key: 'Enter', code: 'Enter', ctrlKey: true })\n        }\n\n        expect(onSubmit).toHaveBeenCalledOnce()\n    })\n\n    test('Enter does not submit when content is empty', async () => {\n        const onSubmit = vi.fn()\n        const { container } = render(PromptEditor, {\n            value: '',\n            onSubmit,\n        })\n\n        const editor = container.querySelector('[data-testid=\"prompt-editor-textarea\"]') as HTMLElement\n        expect(editor).toBeTruthy()\n\n        if (editor) {\n            editor.focus()\n            await fireEvent.keyDown(editor, { key: 'Enter', code: 'Enter' })\n        }\n\n        expect(onSubmit).not.toHaveBeenCalled()\n    })\n\n    test('Shift+Enter does not submit (allows newlines)', async () => {\n        const onSubmit = vi.fn()\n        const { container } = render(PromptEditor, {\n            value: 'test query',\n            onSubmit,\n        })\n\n        const editor = container.querySelector('[data-testid=\"prompt-editor-textarea\"]') as HTMLElement\n        expect(editor).toBeTruthy()\n\n        if (editor) {\n            editor.focus()\n            await fireEvent.keyDown(editor, { key: 'Enter', code: 'Enter', shiftKey: true })\n        }\n\n        expect(onSubmit).not.toHaveBeenCalled()\n    })\n\n    test('Shift+Enter inserts newline', async () => {\n        let value = 'line1'\n        const { container } = render(PromptEditor, {\n            value,\n            onSubmit: vi.fn(),\n        })\n\n        const editor = container.querySelector('[data-testid=\"prompt-editor-textarea\"]') as HTMLElement\n        expect(editor).toBeTruthy()\n\n        if (editor) {\n            editor.focus()\n            await fireEvent.keyDown(editor, { key: 'Enter', code: 'Enter', shiftKey: true })\n\n            // Hard break should be inserted and rendered as line break in DOM\n            // Check that we have the original text and a break was added\n            expect(editor.textContent).toContain('line')\n            expect(editor.textContent).toContain('1')\n        }\n    })\n\n    test('Shift+Enter maintains functionality without breaking editor', async () => {\n        let value = 'line1'\n        const { container } = render(PromptEditor, {\n            value,\n            onSubmit: vi.fn(),\n        })\n\n        const editor = container.querySelector('[data-testid=\"prompt-editor-textarea\"]') as HTMLElement\n        expect(editor).toBeTruthy()\n\n        if (editor) {\n            editor.focus()\n\n            // Insert hard break with Shift+Enter\n            await fireEvent.keyDown(editor, { key: 'Enter', code: 'Enter', shiftKey: true })\n\n            // Verify the editor still functions and has the original text\n            expect(editor.textContent).toContain('line1')\n\n            // Verify we can still type after Shift+Enter (editor should be functional)\n            await fireEvent.keyDown(editor, { key: 'a', code: 'KeyA' })\n            // Editor should still be responsive and contain original text\n            expect(editor.textContent).toContain('line1')\n        }\n    })\n\n    test('Round-trip: loading multi-line text creates proper hard breaks', async () => {\n        // Test the scenario where database text with \\n gets loaded into editor\n        const textWithNewlines = 'line1\\nline2\\nline3'\n\n        const { container } = render(PromptEditor, {\n            value: textWithNewlines,\n            onSubmit: vi.fn(),\n        })\n\n        const editor = container.querySelector('[data-testid=\"prompt-editor-textarea\"]') as HTMLElement\n        expect(editor).toBeTruthy()\n\n        if (editor) {\n            // Verify all lines are present in the DOM\n            expect(editor.textContent).toContain('line1')\n            expect(editor.textContent).toContain('line2')\n            expect(editor.textContent).toContain('line3')\n\n            // Check that hard breaks were created in the DOM structure\n            const proseMirrorDiv = editor.querySelector('[contenteditable=\"true\"]')\n            if (proseMirrorDiv) {\n                const breaks = proseMirrorDiv.querySelectorAll('br')\n                // Should have 2 hard breaks for 3 lines\n                expect(breaks.length).toBeGreaterThan(0)\n            }\n        }\n    })\n\n    test('setText method would handle multi-line text correctly', async () => {\n        // Test the programmatic setting of multi-line text (e.g., from follow-up suggestions)\n        const { container } = render(PromptEditor, {\n            value: '',\n            onSubmit: vi.fn(),\n        })\n\n        const editor = container.querySelector('[data-testid=\"prompt-editor-textarea\"]') as HTMLElement\n        expect(editor).toBeTruthy()\n\n        // In real usage, setText() would be called with multi-line content\n        // and our createParagraphFromText function would handle the conversion\n        const multiLineText = 'question line 1\\nquestion line 2'\n\n        // Verify the editor is ready for such operations\n        expect(editor.textContent).toBeDefined()\n    })\n\n    test('Tab key does not submit and allows default behavior when no suggestions', async () => {\n        const onSubmit = vi.fn()\n        const { container } = render(PromptEditor, {\n            value: 'test query',\n            onSubmit,\n        })\n\n        const editor = container.querySelector('[data-testid=\"prompt-editor-textarea\"]') as HTMLElement\n        expect(editor).toBeTruthy()\n\n        if (editor) {\n            editor.focus()\n            await fireEvent.keyDown(editor, { key: 'Tab', code: 'Tab' })\n        }\n\n        // Tab should not trigger submit when there are no suggestions\n        expect(onSubmit).not.toHaveBeenCalled()\n    })\n})\n","numLines":220,"startLine":1,"totalLines":220}}}

           // Use '\\n' as leafText so hard_break nodes act as barriers (same size as in PM).\n                const textBeforeCursor = $cursor.parent.textBetween(0, parentOffset, undefined, '\\n')\n true,\n                    from: parentStart + result.atPos,\n                    to: sel.from,\n                    query: result.query,\n                }\n            },\n        },","originalFile":"import { keymap } from 'prosemirror-keymap'\nimport { Fragment, Slice } from 'prosemirror-model'\nimport { schema as basicSchema } from 'prosemirror-schema-basic'\nimport { Plugin, PluginKey, TextSelection } from 'prosemirror-state'\nimport { Decoration, DecorationSet } from 'prosemirror-view'\n\n/**\n * Detect @ mention at cursor position\n */\nfunction detectMentionAtCursor(text: string, cursorPos: number): { atPos: number; query: string } | null {\n    // Find latest @ before cursor\n    let atPos = -1\n    for (let i = cursorPos - 1; i >= 0; i--) {\n        if (text[i] === '@') {\n            atPos = i\n            break\n        }\n        // Only break on newlines, allow spaces in mentions\n        if (text[i] === '\\n') {\n            break\n        }\n    }\n\n    if (atPos >= 0) {\n        const afterAt = text.slice(atPos + 1, cursorPos)\n        // Allow spaces in mention queries, only reject newlines\n        if (!afterAt.includes('\\n')) {\n            return { atPos, query: afterAt }\n        }\n    }\n\n    return null\n}\n\n// Coords type for ProseMirror coordsAtPos\ntype Coords = { left: number; right: number; top: number; bottom: number }\n\n// Use basic ProseMirror schema directly\nexport const editorSchema = basicSchema\n\n// Mention state interface\nexport type MentionState = {\n    active: boolean\n    from: number // pos of \"@\"\n    to: number // current cursor\n    query: string\n} | null\n\nexport const mentionKey = new PluginKey<MentionState>('mention')\n\n// Mention detection plugin\nexport function mentionPlugin(opts: { onChange: (state: MentionState, coords?: Coords) => void }): Plugin {\n    return new Plugin<MentionState>({\n        key: mentionKey,\n        state: {\n            init: (): MentionState => null,\n            apply(tr, prev, _oldState, newState): MentionState {\n                const sel = newState.selection as TextSelection\n                if (!sel.empty) return null // Only handle cursor selection\n\n                const text = newState.doc.textBetween(0, newState.doc.content.size, '\\n')\n                const result = detectMentionAtCursor(text, sel.from - 1) // Adjust for paragraph node\n\n                if (!result) return null\n\n                return {\n                    active: true,\n                    from: result.atPos + 1, // Adjust for paragraph node\n                    to: sel.from,\n                    query: result.query,\n                }\n            },\n        },\n\n        props: {\n            decorations(state) {\n                const mention = mentionKey.getState(state)\n                if (!mention || !mention.active) return null\n\n                return DecorationSet.create(state.doc, [\n                    Decoration.inline(mention.from, mention.to, {\n                        class: 'pm-mention-active',\n                    }),\n                ])\n            },\n        },\n\n        view(editorView) {\n            // Check initial state immediately when view is created\n            const initialState = mentionKey.getState(editorView.state)\n\n            if (initialState && initialState.active) {\n                // Get coordinates for the \"@\" character position (from), not cursor (to)\n                try {\n                    const domCoords = editorView.coordsAtPos(initialState.from) as Coords\n                    opts.onChange(initialState, domCoords)\n                } catch (e) {\n                    // In test environment, coordsAtPos might fail - call onChange without coordinates\n                    opts.onChange(initialState, undefined)\n                }\n            }\n\n            return {\n                update(view, prevState) {\n                    const prev = mentionKey.getState(prevState)\n                    const curr = mentionKey.getState(view.state)\n\n                    // Only trigger onChange if state actually changed\n                    if (prev === curr) {\n                        return\n                    }\n\n                    if (curr && curr.active) {\n                        // Get coordinates for the \"@\" character position (from), not cursor (to)\n                        try {\n                            const domCoords = view.coordsAtPos(curr.from) as Coords\n                            opts.onChange(curr, domCoords)\n                        } catch (e) {\n                            // In test environment, coordsAtPos might fail - call onChange without coordinates\n                            opts.onChange(curr, undefined)\n                        }\n                    } else {\n                        opts.onChange(null)\n                    }\n                },\n            }\n        },\n    })\n}\n\n// Helper function to apply suggestion selection\nexport function applySuggestion(view: any, suggestion: string) {\n    const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(view.state)\n    if (!mentionState) return\n\n    const { from, to } = mentionState\n    const newText = suggestion + ' '\n\n    // Safety check: ensure positions are within document bounds\n    if (from < 0 || to > view.state.doc.content.size || from > to) {\n        console.warn('Invalid mention position range:', { from, to, docSize: view.state.doc.content.size })\n        return\n    }\n\n    const tr = view.state.tr.insertText(newText, from, to)\n    const newPos = Math.min(from + newText.length, tr.doc.content.size)\n\n    view.dispatch(tr.setSelection(TextSelection.create(tr.doc, newPos)))\n    view.focus()\n}\n\n// Suggestion interaction interface for keyboard plugin\ninterface SuggestionInteraction {\n    suggestions: Array<any> // Accept any suggestion type as long as it has text property\n    selectedIndex: number\n    selectSuggestion: (suggestion: any) => void\n    hideSuggestions: () => void\n    navigateSelection: (direction: 'up' | 'down') => void\n}\n\n// Comprehensive keyboard handling plugin that integrates mention state with suggestion UI\nexport function keyboardPlugin(opts: {\n    onSubmit?: () => void\n    disabled?: () => boolean\n    getValue?: () => string\n    getSuggestionState?: () => SuggestionInteraction | null\n    onOpenImagePicker?: () => void\n}): Plugin {\n    return keymap({\n        // Handle Arrow keys for suggestion navigation\n        ArrowDown: (_state, _dispatch, view) => {\n            const suggestionState = opts.getSuggestionState?.()\n            if (suggestionState && suggestionState.suggestions.length > 0) {\n                suggestionState.navigateSelection('down')\n                return true // Handled\n            }\n            return false // Let ProseMirror handle\n        },\n\n        ArrowUp: (_state, _dispatch, view) => {\n            const suggestionState = opts.getSuggestionState?.()\n            if (suggestionState && suggestionState.suggestions.length > 0) {\n                suggestionState.navigateSelection('up')\n                return true // Handled\n            }\n            return false // Let ProseMirror handle\n        },\n\n        // Handle Cmd/Ctrl+Enter for form submission\n        'Mod-Enter': (_state, _dispatch, view) => {\n            const value = opts.getValue?.()\n            if (!opts.disabled?.() && value?.trim() && opts.onSubmit) {\n                opts.onSubmit()\n                return true // Handled\n            }\n            return false // Let ProseMirror handle\n        },\n\n        // Handle Enter key for suggestion selection OR form submission\n        Enter: (state, dispatch, view) => {\n            const suggestionState = opts.getSuggestionState?.()\n\n            // If suggestions are active, select the current suggestion\n            if (suggestionState && suggestionState.suggestions.length > 0) {\n                const currentSuggestion = suggestionState.suggestions[suggestionState.selectedIndex]\n                if (currentSuggestion) {\n                    suggestionState.selectSuggestion(currentSuggestion)\n                    return true // Handled\n                }\n            }\n\n            // If no suggestions, handle form submission\n            const value = opts.getValue?.()\n            if (!opts.disabled?.() && value?.trim() && opts.onSubmit) {\n                opts.onSubmit()\n                return true // Handled\n            }\n\n            return false // Let ProseMirror handle (will create new line)\n        },\n\n        // Handle Tab key for suggestion selection\n        Tab: (state, dispatch, view) => {\n            const suggestionState = opts.getSuggestionState?.()\n\n            // If suggestions are active, select the current suggestion with Tab\n            if (suggestionState && suggestionState.suggestions.length > 0) {\n                const currentSuggestion = suggestionState.suggestions[suggestionState.selectedIndex]\n                if (currentSuggestion) {\n                    suggestionState.selectSuggestion(currentSuggestion)\n                    return true // Handled\n                }\n            }\n\n            return false // Let ProseMirror handle default Tab behavior\n        },\n\n        // Handle Shift+Enter for explicit newlines\n        'Shift-Enter': (state, dispatch, view) => {\n            // Use ProseMirror's built-in hard break insertion to properly handle cursor positioning\n            const { hard_break } = state.schema.nodes\n            if (!hard_break || !dispatch) {\n                return false\n            }\n\n            const tr = state.tr.replaceSelectionWith(hard_break.create())\n            dispatch(tr)\n            return true // Prevent submit logic\n        },\n\n        // Handle Escape to close suggestions\n        Escape: (state, dispatch, view) => {\n            // Check if we're in mention mode (regardless of whether suggestions are showing)\n            const pmMention = mentionKey.getState(state) as MentionState | null\n\n            if (!pmMention || !pmMention.active) {\n                return false // Not in mention mode, let ProseMirror handle\n            }\n\n            // Hide suggestions if they're showing\n            const suggestionState = opts.getSuggestionState?.()\n            if (suggestionState) {\n                suggestionState.hideSuggestions()\n            }\n\n            // Delete the entire @mention text (from @ symbol to current cursor position)\n            // This works whether suggestions are showing or not\n            if (dispatch) {\n                const tr = state.tr.delete(pmMention.from, pmMention.to)\n                dispatch(tr)\n            }\n\n            return true // Handled\n        },\n    })\n}\n\nexport interface ImagePluginCallbacks {\n    onFiles: (files: File[]) => void\n}\n\nexport {\n    // Find latest @ before cursor\n    let atPos = -1\n    for (let i = cursorPos - 1; i >= 0; i--) {\n        if (text[i] === '@') {\n            atPos = i\n            break\n        }\n        // Only break on newlines, allow spaces in mentions\n        if (text[i] === '\\n') {\n            break\n        }\n    }\n\n    if (atPos >= 0) {\n        const afterAt = text.slice(atPos + 1, null\n\nexport const mentionKey = new PluginKey<MentionState>('mention')\n\n// Mention detection plugin\nexport function mentionPlugin(opts: { onChange: (state: MentionState, coords?: Coords) => void }): Plugin {\n    return new Plugin<MentionState>({\n        key: mentionKey,\n        state: {\n            init: (): MentionState => null,\n            apply(tr, prev, _oldState, newState): newState.doc.resolve(sel.from)\n                const parentStart = $cursor.start() // PM position of start of parent node's content\n                const parentOffset = $cursor.parentOffset // cursor offset within parent node\n\n                // Get text from start of parent block to cursor.\n                // Use '\\n' as leafText so hard_break nodes act as barriers (same size as in PM).\n                const textBeforeCursor = $cursor.parent.textBetween(0, parentOffset, undefined, '\\n')\n                const result = detectMentionAtCursor(textBeforeCursor, textBeforeCursor.length)\n\n                if (!result) return null\n\n                return {\n                    active: true,\n                    from: parentStart + result.atPos,\n mentionKey.getState(state)\n                if (!mention || !mention.active) return null\n\n                return DecorationSet.create(state.doc, [\n {\n            // Check initial state immediately when view is created\n            const initialState = mentionKey.getState(editorView.state)\n\n            if (initialState && initialState.active) {\n                // Get coordinates for the \"@\" character position (from), not cursor (to)\n                try {\n                    const domCoords = editorView.coordsAtPos(initialState.from) as Coords\n                    opts.onChange(initialState, domCoords)\n                } catch (e) {\n                    // In test environment, coordsAtPos might fail - call onChange without coordinates\n                    opts.onChange(initialState, undefined)\n                }\n            }\n\n            return {\n                update(view, prevState) {\n                    const prev = mentionKey.getState(prevState)\n                    const curr = mentionKey.getState(view.state)\n\n                    // Only trigger onChange if state actually changed\n opts.onChange(curr, undefined)\n                        }\n                    } else {\n                        opts.onChange(null)\n                    }\n                },\n            }\n        },\n    })\n}\n\n// Helper function to apply suggestion selection\nexport function applySuggestion(view: any, suggestion: string) {\n    const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(view.state)\n    if (!mentionState) return\n\n    const { from, to } = mentionState\n    const newText = suggestion + ' '\n\n    // Safety check: ensure positions are within document bounds\n    if (from < 0 || to > view.state.doc.content.size || from > to) {\n        console.warn('Invalid mention position range:', { from, to, docSize: view.state.doc.content.size })\n        return\n    }\n\n    const tr = view.state.tr.insertText(newText, from, to)\n    const newPos = Math.min(from + newText.length, tr.doc.content.size)\n\n    view.dispatch(tr.setSelection(TextSelection.create(tr.doc, newPos)))\n    view.focus()\n}\n\n// Suggestion interaction interface for keyboard plugin\ninterface SuggestionInteraction {\n    suggestions: Array<any> // Accept any suggestion type as long as it has text property\n    selectedIndex: number\n    selectSuggestion: (suggestion: any) => void\n    hideSuggestions: () => void\n    navigateSelection: (direction: 'up' | 'down') => void\n}\n\n// Comprehensive keyboard handling plugin that integrates mention state with suggestion UI\nexport function keyboardPlugin(opts: {\n    onSubmit?: () => void\n    disabled?: () => boolean\n    getValue?: () => string\n    getSuggestionState?: () => SuggestionInteraction | null\n    onOpenImagePicker?: () => void\n}): Plugin {\n    return keymap({\n        // Handle Arrow keys for suggestion navigation\n        ArrowDown: (_state, _dispatch, view) => {\n            const suggestionState = opts.getSuggestionState?.()\n            if (suggestionState && suggestionState.suggestions.length > 0) {\n _dispatch, view) => {\n            const suggestionState = opts.getSuggestionState?.()\n            if (suggestionState && suggestionState.suggestions.length > 0) {\n                suggestionState.navigateSelection('up')\n                return true // Handled\n            }\n            return false // Let ProseMirror handle\n        },\n\n        // Handle Cmd/Ctrl+Enter for form submission\n        'Mod-Enter': (_state, _dispatch, view) => {\n            const value = opts.getValue?.()\n            if (!opts.disabled?.() && value?.trim() && opts.onSubmit) {\n                opts.onSubmit()\n                return true // Handled\n            }\n            return false // Let ProseMirror handle\n        },\n\n        // Handle Enter key for suggestion selection OR form submission\n        Enter: (state, dispatch, view) => {\n            const suggestionState = opts.getSuggestionState?.()\n\n            // If suggestions are active, select the current suggestion\n            if (suggestionState && suggestionState.suggestions.length > 0) {\n                const currentSuggestion = suggestionState.suggestions[suggestionState.selectedIndex]\n                if (currentSuggestion) {\n                    suggestionState.selectSuggestion(currentSuggestion)\n                    return true // Handled\n                }\n            }\n\n            // If no suggestions, handle form submission\n            const value = opts.getValue?.()\n            if (!opts.disabled?.() && value?.trim() && opts.onSubmit) {\n                opts.onSubmit()\n                return true // Handled\n            }\n\n            return false // Let ProseMirror handle (will create new line)\n        },\n\n        // Handle Tab key for suggestion selection\n        Tab: (state, dispatch, view) => {\n            const suggestionState = opts.getSuggestionState?.()\n\n            // If suggestions are active, select the current suggestion with Tab\n            if (suggestionState && suggestionState.suggestions.length > 0) {\n                const currentSuggestion = suggestionState.suggestions[suggestionState.selectedIndex]\n                if (currentSuggestion) {\n                    suggestionState.selectSuggestion(currentSuggestion)\n                    return true // Handled\n                }\n            }\n\n            return false // Let ProseMirror handle default Tab behavior\n        },\n\n        // Handle Shift+Enter for explicit newlines\n        'Shift-Enter': (state, dispatch, view) => {\n            // Use ProseMirror's built-in hard break insertion to properly handle cursor positioning\n            const { hard_break } = state.schema.nodes\n            if (!hard_break || !dispatch) {\n                return false\n            }\n\n            const tr = state.tr.replaceSelectionWith(hard_break.create())\n            dispatch(tr)\n            return true // Prevent submit logic\n        },\n\n        // Handle Escape to close suggestions\n        Escape: (state, dispatch, view) => {\n            // Check if we're in mention mode (regardless of whether suggestions are showing)\n            const pmMention = mentionKey.getState(state) as MentionState | null\n\n            if (!pmMention || !pmMention.active) {\n                return false // Not in mention mode, let ProseMirror handle\n            }\n\n            // Hide suggestions if they're showing\n            const suggestionState = opts.getSuggestionState?.()\n            if (suggestionState) {\n                suggestionState.hideSuggestions()\n            }\n\n            // Delete the entire @mention text (from @ symbol to current cursor position)\n            // This works whether suggestions are showing or not\n            if (dispatch) {\n                const tr = state.tr.delete(pmMention.from, pmMention.to)\n                dispatch(tr)\n            }\n\n            return true // Handled\n        },\n    })\n}\n\nexport interface ImagePluginCallbacks {\n    onFiles: (files: File[]) => void\n}\n\nexport function imagePlugin(callbacks: ImagePluginCallbacks): Plugin {\n    return new Plugin({\n        props: {\n            handlePaste: (_view, event) => {\n                const clipboard = (event as ClipboardEvent).clipboardData\n                if (!clipboard) {\n                    return false\n                }\n\n                const files: File[] = []\n                for (const item of Array.from(clipboard.items)) {\n                    const file = item.getAsFile()\n           callbacks.onFiles(files)\n                    return true\n                }\n\n                return false\n            },\n\n            handleDrop: (_view, event) => {\n                const dataTransfer = (event as DragEvent).dataTransfer\n                if (!dataTransfer || dataTransfer.files.length === 0) {\n                    return false\n                }\n\n                event.preventDefault()\n                callbacks.onFiles(Array.from(dataTransfer.files))\n                return true\n            },\n\n            transformPasted: slice => {\n                const stripFormatting = (frag: Fragment): Fragment =>\n                    Fragment.fromArray(\n                        frag.content\n                            .map(node => {\n {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01VWXAMiEoids8Aej2z7BuAQ","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Ss3oGmjH2SCFCoRU7ESEa4","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/client/web-sveltekit/src/routes/(app)/deepsearch/PromptEditor.plugins.test.ts","content":"import { schema as basicSchema } from 'prosemirror-schema-basic'\nimport { EditorState, TextSelection } from 'prosemirror-state'\nimport { describe, expect, test } from 'vitest'\n\nimport { detectMentionAtCursor, editorSchema, mentionKey, mentionPlugin } from './PromptEditor.plugins'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Build a ProseMirror EditorState from a plain string.\n * Newlines in the string become hard_break nodes inside a single paragraph.\n * The cursor is placed at the very end of the content.\n */\nfunction stateFromText(text: string, cursorAtEnd = true): EditorState {\n    const schema = editorSchema\n\n    // Build inline content from a single-line string (no newlines expected here).\n    const buildInlineContent = (line: string) => {\n        if (line.length === 0) return []\n        return [schema.text(line)]\n    }\n\n    // Split on newlines and interleave hard_break nodes.\n    const lines = text.split('\\n')\n    const inlineNodes: ReturnType<typeof schema.text>[] = []\n    for (let i = 0; i < lines.length; i++) {\n        inlineNodes.push(...buildInlineContent(lines[i]))\n        if (i < lines.length - 1) {\n            inlineNodes.push(schema.node('hard_break'))\n        }\n    }\n\n    const para = schema.node('paragraph', null, inlineNodes.length > 0 ? inlineNodes : [])\n    const doc = schema.node('doc', null, [para])\n\n    const state = EditorState.create({\n        doc,\n        plugins: [mentionPlugin({ onChange: () => {} })],\n    })\n\n    if (cursorAtEnd) {\n        // Place cursor at the very end (inside the paragraph, after last character).\n        const endPos = doc.content.size - 1 // last position inside the paragraph\n        const tr = state.tr.setSelection(TextSelection.create(state.doc, endPos))\n        return state.apply(tr)\n    }\n\n    return state\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a multi-paragraph EditorState. Each element of `paragraphs` becomes\n * a separate paragraph node. The cursor is placed at the end of the last paragraph.\n */\nfunction stateFromParagraphs(paragraphs: string[]): EditorState {\n    const schema = editorSchema\n\n    const paraNodes = paragraphs.map(text => {\n        const content = text.length > 0 ? [schema.text(text)] : []\n        return schema.node('paragraph', null, content)\n    })\n\n    const doc = schema.node('doc', null, paraNodes)\n\n    const state = EditorState.create({\n        doc,\n        plugins: [mentionPlugin({ onChange: () => {} })],\n    })\n\n    // Place cursor at the end of the last paragraph.\n    const lastParaEnd = doc.content.size - 1\n    const tr = state.tr.setSelection(TextSelection.create(state.doc, lastParaEnd))\n    return state.apply(tr)\n}\n\n/**\n * Place the cursor immediately after a character sequence within a state.\n * Returns the new state with the cursor positioned after the first occurrence\n * of `marker` in the document text (absolute PM position).\n */\nfunction stateWithCursorAfter(state: EditorState, marker: string): EditorState {\n    const fullText = state.doc.textContent\n    const idx = fullText.indexOf(marker)\n    if (idx === -1) throw new Error(`Marker \"${marker}\" not found in document`)\n\n    // We need to find the absolute PM position of the character after the marker.\n    // Walk through the doc to map flat text offset to PM position.\n    let flatOffset = 0\n    let pmPos = -1\n    state.doc.nodesBetween(0, state.doc.content.size, (node, pos) => {\n        if (node.isText) {\n            const text = node.text!\n            for (let i = 0; i < text.length; i++) {\n                if (flatOffset === idx + marker.length - 1) {\n                    pmPos = pos + i + 1 // position AFTER this character\n                }\n                flatOffset++\n            }\n        }\n    })\n\n    if (pmPos === -1) throw new Error(`Could not locate PM position for marker \"${marker}\"`)\n    const tr = state.tr.setSelection(TextSelection.create(state.doc, pmPos))\n    return state.apply(tr)\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// detectMentionAtCursor – unit tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('detectMentionAtCursor', () => {\n    test('returns null when no @ present', () => {\n        expect(detectMentionAtCursor('hello world', 11)).toBeNull()\n    })\n\n    test('detects @ at the start of text', () => {\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('@repo', 5)\n        expect(result).toEqual({ atPos: 0, query: 'repo' })\n    })\n\n    test('detects @ in the middle of text', () => {\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('hello @repo', 11)\n        expect(result).toEqual({ atPos: 6, query: 'repo' })\n    })\n\n    test('detects @ with empty query (cursor right after @)', () => {\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('hello @', 7)\n        expect(result).toEqual({ atPos: 6, query: '' })\n    })\n\n    test('detects @ with spaces in query', () => {\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('hello @my repo', 14)\n        expect(result).toEqual({ atPos: 6, query: 'my repo' })\n    })\n\n    test('does not cross newline boundary', () => {\n        // Newline before @ means the @ is on a previous \"line\" and should not be detected\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('@repo\\nhello', 11)\n        expect(result).toBeNull()\n    })\n\n    test('detects @ on a line after a newline', () => {\n        // Simulates hard_break producing '\\n' before '@'\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('first line\\n@repo', 16)\n        expect(result).toEqual({ atPos: 11, query: 'repo' })\n    })\n\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Ss3oGmjH2SCFCoRU7ESEa4","type":"tool_result","content":"File created successfully at: /workspace/client/web-sveltekit/src/routes/(app)/deepsearch/PromptEditor.plugins.test.ts"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bf197e93-71cd-45cd-91ed-ec6ce98b4931","uuid":"b1b95009-74b4-4efc-b137-9f0b9ffec92d","tool_use_result":{"type":"create","filePath":"/workspace/client/web-sveltekit/src/routes/(app)/deepsearch/PromptEditor.plugins.test.ts","content":"import { schema as basicSchema } from 'prosemirror-schema-basic'\nimport { EditorState, TextSelection } from 'prosemirror-state'\nimport { describe, expect, test } from 'vitest'\n\nimport { detectMentionAtCursor, editorSchema, mentionKey, mentionPlugin } from './PromptEditor.plugins'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Build a ProseMirror EditorState from a plain string.\n * Newlines in the string become hard_break nodes inside a single paragraph.\n * The cursor is placed at the very end of the content.\n */\nfunction stateFromText(text: string, cursorAtEnd = true): EditorState {\n    const schema = editorSchema\n\n    // Build inline content from a single-line string (no newlines expected here).\n    const buildInlineContent = (line: string) => {\n        if (line.length === 0) return []\n        return [schema.text(line)]\n    }\n\n    // Split on newlines and interleave hard_break nodes.\n    const lines = text.split('\\n')\n    const inlineNodes: ReturnType<typeof schema.text>[] = []\n    for (let i = 0; i < lines.length; i++) {\n        inlineNodes.push(...buildInlineContent(lines[i]))\n        if (i < lines.length - 1) {\n            inlineNodes.push(schema.node('hard_break'))\n        }\n    }\n\n    const para = schema.node('paragraph', null, inlineNodes.length > 0 ? inlineNodes : [])\n    const doc = schema.node('doc', null, [para])\n\n    const state = EditorState.create({\n        doc,\n        plugins: [mentionPlugin({ onChange: () => {} })],\n    })\n\n    if (cursorAtEnd) {\n        // Place cursor at the very end (inside the paragraph, after last character).\n        const endPos = doc.content.size - 1 // last position inside the paragraph\n        const tr = state.tr.setSelection(TextSelection.create(state.doc, endPos))\n        return state.apply(tr)\n    }\n\n    return state\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a multi-paragraph EditorState. Each element of `paragraphs` becomes\n * a separate paragraph node. The cursor is placed at the end of the last paragraph.\n */\nfunction stateFromParagraphs(paragraphs: string[]): EditorState {\n    const schema = editorSchema\n\n    const paraNodes = paragraphs.map(text => {\n        const content = text.length > 0 ? [schema.text(text)] : []\n        return schema.node('paragraph', null, content)\n    })\n\n    const doc = schema.node('doc', null, paraNodes)\n\n    const state = EditorState.create({\n        doc,\n        plugins: [mentionPlugin({ onChange: () => {} })],\n    })\n\n    // Place cursor at the end of the last paragraph.\n    const lastParaEnd = doc.content.size - 1\n    const tr = state.tr.setSelection(TextSelection.create(state.doc, lastParaEnd))\n    return state.apply(tr)\n}\n\n/**\n * Place the cursor immediately after a character sequence within a state.\n * Returns the new state with the cursor positioned after the first occurrence\n * of `marker` in the document text (absolute PM position).\n */\nfunction stateWithCursorAfter(state: EditorState, marker: string): EditorState {\n    const fullText = state.doc.textContent\n    const idx = fullText.indexOf(marker)\n    if (idx === -1) throw new Error(`Marker \"${marker}\" not found in document`)\n\n    // We need to find the absolute PM position of the character after the marker.\n    // Walk through the doc to map flat text offset to PM position.\n    let flatOffset = 0\n    let pmPos = -1\n    state.doc.nodesBetween(0, state.doc.content.size, (node, pos) => {\n        if (node.isText) {\n            const text = node.text!\n            for (let i = 0; i < text.length; i++) {\n                if (flatOffset === idx + marker.length - 1) {\n                    pmPos = pos + i + 1 // position AFTER this character\n                }\n                flatOffset++\n            }\n        }\n    })\n\n    if (pmPos === -1) throw new Error(`Could not locate PM position for marker \"${marker}\"`)\n    const tr = state.tr.setSelection(TextSelection.create(state.doc, pmPos))\n    return state.apply(tr)\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// detectMentionAtCursor – unit tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('detectMentionAtCursor', () => {\n    test('returns null when no @ present', () => {\n        expect(detectMentionAtCursor('hello world', 11)).toBeNull()\n    })\n\n    test('detects @ at the start of text', () => {\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('@repo', 5)\n        expect(result).toEqual({ atPos: 0, query: 'repo' })\n    })\n\n    test('detects @ in the middle of text', () => {\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('hello @repo', 11)\n        expect(result).toEqual({ atPos: 6, query: 'repo' })\n    })\n\n    test('detects @ with empty query (cursor right after @)', () => {\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('hello @', 7)\n        expect(result).toEqual({ atPos: 6, query: '' })\n    })\n\n    test('detects @ with spaces in query', () => {\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('hello @my repo', 14)\n        expect(result).toEqual({ atPos: 6, query: 'my repo' })\n    })\n\n    test('does not cross newline boundary', () => {\n        // Newline before @ means the @ is on a previous \"line\" and should not be detected\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('@repo\\nhello', 11)\n        expect(result).toBeNull()\n    })\n\n    test('detects @ on a line after a newline', () => {\n        // Simulates hard_break producing '\\n' before '@'\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('first line\\n@repo', 16)\n        expect(result).toEqual({ atPos: 11, query: 'repo' })\n    })\n\n    test('returns null when @ is before a newline and cursor is after the newline', () => {\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('@repo\\nhello', 11)\n        expect(result).toBeNull()\n    })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// mentionPlugin state – integration tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('mentionPlugin state', () => {\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n expect(mentionState!.active).toBe(true)\n        expect(mentionState!.query).toBe('repo')\n\n        // Verify from points to the '@' character in the document.\n        // doc: [para_open(0), 'hello @repo'(1-11), para_close], '@' is at position 7.\n        const docText = state.doc.textContent\n        expect(docText[mentionState!.from - 1]).toBe('@')\n    })\n\n    test('single-line: @ at start of text has from=1 (first char inside para)', () => {\n        const state = stateFromText('@myrepo')\n        const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(state)\n\n        expect(mentionState).not.toBeNull()\n        expect(mentionState!.query).toBe('myrepo')\n        // '@' is the very first character inside the paragraph (position 1)\n        expect(mentionState!.from).toBe(1)\n    })\n\n    test('single-line: partial query (cursor mid-word)', () => {\n        // Build state with cursor after \"@re\" (not end of \"@repo\")\n        const baseState = stateFromText('@repo', false)\n        const state = stateWithCursorAfter(baseState, '@re')\n        const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(state)\n\n        expect(mentionState).not.toBeNull()\n        expect(mentionState!.query).toBe('re')\n    })\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Multi-paragraph scenarios\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    test('multi-paragraph: cursor in second paragraph, @ detected with correct position', () => {\n        const state = stateFromParagraphs(['first paragraph', '@repo'])\n        const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(state)\n\n        expect(mentionState).not.toBeNull()\n        expect(mentionState!.active).toBe(true)\n        expect(mentionState!.query).toBe('repo')\n\n        // The '@' must be inside the second paragraph.\n        // Verify by resolving the position: the character at from should be '@'.\n        const resolvedAt = state.doc.resolve(mentionState!.from)\n        expect(resolvedAt.parent.textContent[resolvedAt.parentOffset]).toBe('@')\n    })\n\n    test('multi-paragraph: cursor in third paragraph, position is correct', () => {\n        const state = stateFromParagraphs(['para one', 'para two', '@hello world'])\n        const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(state)\n\n        expect(mentionState).not.toBeNull()\n        expect(mentionState!.query).toBe('hello world')\n\n        const resolvedAt = state.doc.resolve(mentionState!.from)\n        expect(resolvedAt.parent.textContent[resolvedAt.parentOffset]).toBe('@')\n    })\n\n    test('multi-paragraph: no @ in last paragraph → null', () => {\n        const state = stateFromParagraphs(['@mention in first', 'plain second'])\n        const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(state)\n        expect(mentionState).toBeNull()\n    })\n\n    test('multi-paragraph: @ in The assertion is checking `resolvedAt.parent.textContent[resolvedAt.parentOffset]` but for a hard_break scenario in a single paragraph, the paragraph contains both text and a hard_break node.\n\nLet me debug this. The `stateFromText` function with \"before\\n@repo\":\n- Splits on '\\n' to get [\"before\", \"@repo\"]\n- Creates a paragraph with: schema.text(\"before\"), hard_break, schema.text(\"@repo\")\n\nThe paragraph text content is \"before@repo\" (hard_break doesn't appear in `textContent`).\n\nWhen I do `$cursor.parent.textBetween(0, parentOffset, undefined, '\\n')`:\n\n\n- It gives \"before\\n@repo\"\n- '@' is at flat position 7 (0-indexed: b=0, e=1, f=2, o=3, r=4, e=5, \\n=6, @=7)\n\n`result.atPos = 7`\n`from = parentStart + atPos = parentStart + 7`\n\n`parentStart` is the PM start of the paragraph's content (position 1).\nSo `from = 1 + 7 = 8`.\n\nBut the paragraph contains:\n- \"before\" (6 chars): positions 1-6\n- hard_break (nodeSize=1): position 7\n- \"@repo\" starts at position 8\n\nSo position 8 is actually the start of the second text node, not the '@' character itself.\n\nWhen I resolve position 8, the parent offset becomes 7 (since the parent starts at 1), and looking at the parent's textContent which is \"before@repo\" (the hard_break is excluded), position 7 gives me 'r' instead of '@'. This is the off-by-one error—I'm calculating the position in the flat text but then using it as an index into the parent's textContent, which has a different structure. The issue is that `textContent` excludes the hard_break node, so the character indices don't align with ProseMirror's position system. I need a better way to verify the '@' character—using `doc.textBetween()` to extract the actual character at the mention position would be more reliable than relying on `textContent` indexing. state.doc.textBetween(P, P+1, undefined, '')`\n\nSo `textBetween(8, 9)` would return the '@' character. Let me trace through the old code to understand what `mentionState.from` actually represents. The `from` value comes from `result.atPos + 1`, where `atPos` is the 0-indexed position in the flat text. For \"@repo\" in a single paragraph, that's position 0, so `from` becomes 1, which in ProseMirror terms points to the gap right before the '@' character. This means I need `textBetween(from, from+1)` to extract that character.\n\nThe new code follows the same logic: `from: parentStart + result.atPos` gives me the same position. I should verify the `query` is correct and double-check the `from` and `to` values rather than getting bogged down in character extraction details. expect(charAtFrom).toBe('@')\n    })","originalFile":"import { schema as basicSchema } from 'prosemirror-schema-basic'\nimport { EditorState, TextSelection } from 'prosemirror-state'\nimport { describe, expect, test } from 'vitest'\n\nimport { detectMentionAtCursor, editorSchema, mentionKey, mentionPlugin } from './PromptEditor.plugins'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Build a ProseMirror EditorState from a plain string.\n * Newlines in the string become hard_break nodes inside a single paragraph.\n * The cursor is placed at the very end of the content.\n */\nfunction stateFromText(text: string, cursorAtEnd = true): EditorState {\n    const schema = editorSchema\n\n    // Build inline content from a single-line string (no newlines expected here).\n    const buildInlineContent = (line: string) => {\n        if (line.length === 0) return []\n        return [schema.text(line)]\n    }\n\n    // Split on newlines and interleave hard_break nodes.\n    const lines = text.split('\\n')\n    const inlineNodes: ReturnType<typeof schema.text>[] = []\n    for (let i = 0; i < lines.length; i++) {\n        inlineNodes.push(...buildInlineContent(lines[i]))\n        if (i < lines.length - 1) {\n            inlineNodes.push(schema.node('hard_break'))\n        }\n    }\n\n    const para = schema.node('paragraph', null, inlineNodes.length > 0 ? inlineNodes : [])\n    const doc = schema.node('doc', null, [para])\n\n    const state = EditorState.create({\n        doc,\n        plugins: [mentionPlugin({ onChange: () => {} })],\n    })\n\n    if (cursorAtEnd) {\n        // Place cursor at the very end (inside the paragraph, after last character).\n        const endPos = doc.content.size - 1 // last position inside the paragraph\n        const tr = state.tr.setSelection(TextSelection.create(state.doc, endPos))\n        return state.apply(tr)\n    }\n\n    return state\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a multi-paragraph EditorState. Each element of `paragraphs` becomes\n * a separate paragraph node. The cursor is placed at the end of the last paragraph.\n */\nfunction stateFromParagraphs(paragraphs: string[]): EditorState {\n    const schema = editorSchema\n\n    const paraNodes = paragraphs.map(text => {\n        const content = text.length > 0 ? [schema.text(text)] : []\n        return schema.node('paragraph', null, content)\n    })\n\n    const doc = schema.node('doc', null, paraNodes)\n\n    const state = EditorState.create({\n        doc,\n        plugins: [mentionPlugin({ onChange: () => {} })],\n    })\n\n    // Place cursor at the end of the last paragraph.\n    const lastParaEnd = doc.content.size - 1\n    const tr = state.tr.setSelection(TextSelection.create(state.doc, lastParaEnd))\n    return state.apply(tr)\n}\n\n/**\n * Place the cursor immediately after a character sequence within a state.\n * Returns the new state with the cursor positioned after the first occurrence\n * of `marker` in the document text (absolute PM position).\n */\nfunction stateWithCursorAfter(state: EditorState, marker: string): EditorState {\n    const fullText = state.doc.textContent\n    const idx = fullText.indexOf(marker)\n    if (idx === -1) throw new Error(`Marker \"${marker}\" not found in document`)\n\n    // We need to find the absolute PM position of the character after the marker.\n    // Walk through the doc to map flat text offset to PM position.\n    let flatOffset = 0\n    let pmPos = -1\n    state.doc.nodesBetween(0, state.doc.content.size, (node, pos) => {\n        if (node.isText) {\n            const text = node.text!\n            for (let i = 0; i < text.length; i++) {\n                if (flatOffset === idx + marker.length - 1) {\n                    pmPos = pos + i + 1 // position AFTER this character\n                }\n                flatOffset++\n            }\n        }\n    })\n\n    if (pmPos === -1) throw new Error(`Could not locate PM position for marker \"${marker}\"`)\n    const tr = state.tr.setSelection(TextSelection.create(state.doc, pmPos))\n    return state.apply(tr)\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// detectMentionAtCursor – unit tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('detectMentionAtCursor', () => {\n    test('returns null when no @ present', () => {\n        expect(detectMentionAtCursor('hello world', 11)).toBeNull()\n    })\n\n    test('detects @ at the start of text', () => {\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('@repo', 5)\n        expect(result).toEqual({ atPos: 0, query: 'repo' })\n    })\n\n    test('detects @ in the middle of text', () => {\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('hello @repo', 11)\n        expect(result).toEqual({ atPos: 6, query: 'repo' })\n    })\n\n    test('detects @ with empty query (cursor right after @)', () => {\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('hello @', 7)\n        expect(result).toEqual({ atPos: 6, query: '' })\n    })\n\n    test('detects @ with spaces in query', () => {\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('hello @my repo', 14)\n        expect(result).toEqual({ atPos: 6, query: 'my repo' })\n    })\n\n    test('does not cross newline boundary', () => {\n        // Newline before @ means the @ is on a previous \"line\" and should not be detected\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('@repo\\nhello', 11)\n        expect(result).toBeNull()\n    })\n\n    test('detects @ on a line after a newline', () => {\n        // Simulates hard_break producing '\\n' before '@'\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('first line\\n@repo', 16)\n        expect(result).toEqual({ atPos: 11, query: 'repo' })\n    })\n\n    test('returns null when @ is before a newline and cursor is after the newline', () => {\n        const result = detectMentionAtCursor('@repo\\nhello', 11)\n        expect(result).toBeNull()\n    })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// mentionPlugin state – integration tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('mentionPlugin state', () => {\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Single-paragraph scenarios\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    test('single-line: no mention → state is null', () => {\n        const state = stateFromText('hello world')\n        const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(state)\n        expect(mentionState).toBeNull()\n    })\n\n    test('single-line: detects mention with correct from/to positions', () => {\n        const state = stateFromText('hello @repo')\n        const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(state)\n\n        expect(mentionState).not.toBeNull()\n        expect(mentionState!.active).toBe(true)\n        expect(mentionState!.query).toBe('repo')\n\n        // Verify from points to the '@' character in the document.\n        // doc: [para_open(0), 'hello @repo'(1-11), para_close], '@' is at position 7.\n        const docText = state.doc.textContent\n        expect(docText[mentionState!.from - 1]).toBe('@')\n    })\n\n    test('single-line: @ at start of text has from=1 (first char inside para)', () => {\n        const state = stateFromText('@myrepo')\n        const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(state)\n\n        expect(mentionState).not.toBeNull()\n        expect(mentionState!.query).toBe('myrepo')\n        // '@' is the very first character inside the paragraph (position 1)\n        expect(mentionState!.from).toBe(1)\n    })\n\n    test('single-line: partial query (cursor mid-word)', () => {\n        // Build state with cursor after \"@re\" (not end of \"@repo\")\n        const baseState = stateFromText('@repo', false)\n        const state = stateWithCursorAfter(baseState, '@re')\n        const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(state)\n\n        expect(mentionState).not.toBeNull()\n        expect(mentionState!.query).toBe('re')\n    })\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Multi-paragraph scenarios\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    test('multi-paragraph: cursor in second paragraph, @ detected with correct position', () => {\n        const state = stateFromParagraphs(['first paragraph', '@repo'])\n        const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(state)\n\n        expect(mentionState).not.toBeNull()\n        expect(mentionState!.active).toBe(true)\n        expect(mentionState!.query).toBe('repo')\n\n        // The '@' must be inside the second paragraph.\n        // Verify by resolving the position: the character at from should be '@'.\n        const resolvedAt = state.doc.resolve(mentionState!.from)\n        expect(resolvedAt.parent.textContent[resolvedAt.parentOffset]).toBe('@')\n    })\n\n    test('multi-paragraph: cursor in third paragraph, position is correct', () => {\n        const state = stateFromParagraphs(['para one', 'para two', '@hello world'])\n        const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(state)\n\n        expect(mentionState).not.toBeNull()\n        expect(mentionState!.query).toBe('hello world')\n\n        const resolvedAt = state.doc.resolve(mentionState!.from)\n        expect(resolvedAt.parent.textContent[resolvedAt.parentOffset]).toBe('@')\n    })\n\n    test('multi-paragraph: no @ in last paragraph → null', () => {\n        const state = stateFromParagraphs(['@mention in first', 'plain second'])\n        const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(state)\n        expect(mentionState).toBeNull()\n    })\n\n    test('multi-paragraph: @ in first paragraph does not bleed into second', () => {\n        // Cursor is at end of second paragraph (no @)\n        const state = stateFromParagraphs(['@first', 'second'])\n        const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(state)\n        expect(mentionState).toBeNull()\n    })\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Hard-break (Shift+Enter) scenarios\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    test('hard-break: @ after hard-break is detected with correct position', () => {\n        // Single paragraph containing a hard_break before '@repo'\n        const state = stateFromText('before\\n@repo')\n        const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(state)\n\n        expect(mentionState).not.toBeNull()\n        expect(mentionState!.query).toBe('repo')\n\n        // Verify the 'from' position resolves to '@' in the document.\n        const resolvedAt = state.doc.resolve(mentionState!.from)\n        expect(resolvedAt.parent.textContent[resolvedAt.parentOffset]).toBe('@')\n    })\n\n    test('hard-break: @ before hard-break is not detected when cursor is after the break', () => {\n        // Cursor is at the end of \"after\" (the line after the hard_break).\n        // '@before' is on the first line, so mention detection should stop at the hard_break.\n        const baseState = stateFromText('@before\\nafter')\n        // Move cursor to the end (which is already after \"after\")\n        const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(baseState)\n        expect(mentionState).toBeNull()\n    })\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Edge cases\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    test('@ at start of second paragraph', () => {\n        const state = stateFromParagraphs(['', '@start'])\n        const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(state)\n\n        expect(mentionState).not.toBeNull()\n        expect(mentionState!.query).toBe('start')\n    })\n\n    test('@ after whitespace', () => {\n        const state = stateFromText('   @spaced')\n        const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(state)\n\n        expect(mentionState).not.toBeNull()\n        expect(mentionState!.query).toBe('spaced')\n    })\n\n    test('empty document has no mention', () => {\n        const state = stateFromText('')\n        const mentionState = mentionKey.getState(state)\n        expect(mentionState).toBeNull()\n You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bf197e93-71cd-45cd-91ed-ec6ce98b4931","uuid":"ee88fb6c-c0a6-4071-9945-2b434c86f457","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/workspace/client/web-sveltekit/src/routes/(app)/deepsearch/PromptEditor.plugins.ts","content":"import { keymap } from 'prosemirror-keymap'\nimport { Fragment, Slice } from 'prosemirror-model'\nimport { schema as basicSchema } from 'prosemirror-schema-basic'\nimport { Plugin, PluginKey, TextSelection } from 'prosemirror-state'\nimport { Decoration, DecorationSet } from 'prosemirror-view'\n\n/**\n * Detect @ mention at cursor position\n */\nexport function detectMentionAtCursor(text: string, cursorPos: number): { atPos: number; query: string } | null {\n    // Find latest @ before cursor\n    let atPos = -1\n    for (let i = cursorPos - 1; i >= 0; i--) {\n        if (text[i] === '@') {\n            atPos = i\n            break\n        }\n        // Only break on newlines, allow spaces in mentions\n        if (text[i] === '\\n') {\n            break\n        }\n    }\n\n    if (atPos >= 0) {\n        const afterAt = text.slice(atPos + 1, cursorPos)\n        // Allow spaces in mention queries, only reject newlines\n        if (!afterAt.includes('\\n')) {\n            return { atPos, query: afterAt }\n        }\n    }\n\n    return null\n}\n\n// Coords type for ProseMirror coordsAtPos\ntype Coords = { left: number; right: number; top: number; bottom: number }\n\n// Use basic ProseMirror schema directly\nexport const editorSchema = basicSchema\n\n// Mention state interface\nexport type MentionState = {\n    active: boolean\n    from: number // pos of \"@\"\n    to: number // current cursor\n    query: string\n} | null\n\nexport const mentionKey = new PluginKey<MentionState>('mention')\n\n// Mention detection plugin\nexport function mentionPlugin(opts: { onChange: (state: MentionState, coords?: Coords) => void }): Plugin {\n    return new Plugin<MentionState>({\n        key: mentionKey,\n        state: {\n            init: (): MentionState => null,\n            apply(tr, prev, _oldState, newState): MentionState {\n                const sel = newState.selection as TextSelection\n                if (!sel.empty) return null // Only handle cursor selection\n\n                const $cursor = newState.doc.resolve(sel.from)\n                const parentStart = $cursor.start() // PM position of start of parent node's content\n                const parentOffset = $cursor.parentOffset // cursor offset within parent node\n\n                // Get text from start of parent block to cursor.\n                // Use '\\n' as leafText so hard_break nodes act as barriers (same size as in PM).\n                const textBeforeCursor = $cursor.parent.textBetween(0, parentOffset, undefined, '\\n')\n                const result = detectMentionAtCursor(textBeforeCursor, textBeforeCursor.length)\n\n                if (!result) return null\n\n                return {\n                    active: true,\n                    from: parentStart + result.atPos,\n                    to: sel.from,\n                    query: result.query,\n                }\n            },\n        },\n","numLines":80,"startLine":1,"totalLines":344}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01M3bp1s5eT65bHQMmusoCmi","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Here's a summary of what was done:\n\n## Problem\n\nThe `mentionPlugin`'s `state.apply` function computed the `@` anchor position by:\n1. Flattening the entire document with `doc.textBetween(0, docSize, '\\n')`\n2. Using `sel.from - 1` as the cursor position in that flat string\n3. Mapping back with `result.atPos + 1`\n\nThis only works for single-paragraph documents. For N paragraphs, each paragraph boundary adds 2 ProseMirror tokens (open + close) but only 1 flat-text character (the `\\n` separator), causing the offset to drift by 1 per additional paragraph.\n\n## Fix (`PromptEditor.plugins.ts`)\n\nInstead of working with the full document, the fix:\n1. **Resolves the cursor position** with `doc.resolve(sel.from)` to get `$cursor`\n2. **Gets the parent block's start** with `$cursor.start()` (the PM position right after the paragraph's opening token)\n3. **Gets text within the current block only** with `$cursor.parent.textBetween(0, parentOffset, undefined, '\\n')` — using `'\\n'` as `leafText` so `hard_break` nodes contribute exactly 1 character (matching their PM nodeSize of 1)\n4. **Computes `from`** as `parentStart + result.atPos` — always correct regardless of how many paragraphs precede the current one\n\n## Tests (`PromptEditor.plugins.test.ts`)\n\n21 tests covering:\n- `detectMentionAtCursor` unit tests (8 cases): no `@`, `@` at start, mid-text, empty query, spaces in query, newline barrier\n- `mentionPlugin` state integration tests (13 cases): single-line, partial query, multi-paragraph (2nd and 3rd paragraph), no bleed-across, hard-break detection, edge cases (`@` at start of paragraph, `@` after whitespace, empty doc)"}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1572,"cache_read_input_tokens":67504,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1572,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bf197e93-71cd-45cd-91ed-ec6ce98b4931","uuid":"52f4aab3-400f-48b7-a9d7-00ddb43b693c"}
